questions.question: Prove that the sum of the interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360°.
questions.answer: A quadrilateral can be divided into two triangles by drawing one diagonal. Since the sum of the angles of each triangle is 180°, the sum of the interior angles of the quadrilateral is 180° + 180° = 360°.

questions.question: Prove that opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: In parallelogram ABCD, draw diagonal AC. Triangles ABC and CDA are formed. Since AB ∥ CD and BC ∥ AD, alternate interior angles are equal. Thus triangles ABC and CDA are congruent, so opposite sides AB = CD and BC = AD.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.
questions.answer: In parallelogram ABCD, diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. Consider triangles AOB and COD. Using parallel sides, alternate interior angles are equal and opposite sides are equal. Thus triangles are congruent, giving AO = OC and BO = OD.

questions.question: Prove that opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: In parallelogram ABCD, AB ∥ CD and AD ∥ BC. Using alternate interior angles formed by transversals, angles A = C and angles B = D.

questions.question: Prove that adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary.
questions.answer: In parallelogram ABCD, AB ∥ CD. Angle A and angle B lie on the same side of the transversal AD. Hence they form a linear pair and their sum is 180°.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle are equal.
questions.answer: In rectangle ABCD, angles are 90°. Consider triangles ABC and BAD. AB is common, BC = AD, and angles are right angles. Thus triangles are congruent and AC = BD.

questions.question: Explain why every square is a rectangle.
questions.answer: A square has all angles equal to 90° and opposite sides parallel. Since a rectangle is defined as a quadrilateral with four right angles, every square satisfies this definition.

questions.question: Explain why every rectangle is a parallelogram.
questions.answer: In a rectangle, opposite sides are parallel and equal. This satisfies the definition of a parallelogram.

questions.question: Find the missing angle of a quadrilateral if three angles are 80°, 90°, and 110°.
questions.answer: Sum of quadrilateral angles = 360°. Missing angle = 360 − (80 + 90 + 110) = 80°.

questions.question: Explain the properties of a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Opposite sides are parallel and equal, opposite angles are equal, adjacent angles are supplementary, and diagonals bisect each other.

questions.question: Explain the properties of a rectangle.
questions.answer: All angles are 90°, opposite sides are equal and parallel, and diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

questions.question: Explain the properties of a square.
questions.answer: All sides are equal, all angles are 90°, diagonals are equal and bisect each other at right angles.

questions.question: Explain why the diagonals of a square intersect at right angles.
questions.answer: Since a square is both a rectangle and a rhombus, its diagonals bisect each other and meet at right angles.

questions.question: Explain why opposite sides of a rectangle are equal.
questions.answer: A rectangle is a parallelogram with right angles, so opposite sides are equal due to parallelogram properties.

questions.question: Explain why the diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.
questions.answer: When diagonals intersect, they divide each other into two equal parts due to triangle congruence formed within the parallelogram.

questions.question: Explain why the diagonals of a rectangle are equal.
questions.answer: Both diagonals span across equal opposite sides and form congruent triangles, making their lengths equal.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a rectangle and a square.
questions.answer: A rectangle has opposite sides equal and all angles 90°, while a square has all sides equal in addition to having all angles 90°.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a parallelogram and a rectangle.
questions.answer: A parallelogram has opposite sides parallel and equal but angles are not necessarily 90°, whereas a rectangle has all angles equal to 90°.

questions.question: Explain why adjacent angles of a parallelogram sum to 180°.
questions.answer: Adjacent angles lie on the same side of a transversal between parallel sides, forming supplementary angles.

questions.question: Explain why the diagonals of a square are equal.
questions.answer: Since a square is a special type of rectangle, its diagonals are equal.

questions.question: Explain why opposite sides of a parallelogram are parallel.
questions.answer: By definition, a parallelogram is a quadrilateral with both pairs of opposite sides parallel.

questions.question: Explain the role of diagonals in quadrilaterals.
questions.answer: Diagonals divide quadrilaterals into triangles and help analyze properties such as congruence and symmetry.

questions.question: Explain how a quadrilateral can be divided into triangles.
questions.answer: By drawing one diagonal between opposite vertices, a quadrilateral can be divided into two triangles.

questions.question: Explain why the sum of the interior angles of a rectangle is 360°.
questions.answer: Since a rectangle is a quadrilateral and each angle is 90°, the total is 90 × 4 = 360°.

questions.question: Explain the classification of quadrilaterals.
questions.answer: Quadrilaterals are classified into parallelograms, rectangles, squares, rhombuses, and trapeziums based on their sides and angles.
